detections.title: Failed Logon Burst From One Source
detections.threshold: 5 events in 5 minutes per source and computer
detections.severity_reason: Repeated failures from one source are useful early signal but need ownership and scanner context before escalation.
detections.tuning: Baseline trusted scanners and identity infrastructure before adding exclusions.

detections.title: Failed Logons Across Multiple Accounts
detections.threshold: 4 distinct users in 10 minutes per source and computer
detections.severity_reason: Failures across several accounts increase concern, especially when the source is not an approved gateway or test system.
detections.tuning: Review shared gateways and authorized identity-testing systems.

detections.title: Successful Logon After Repeated Failures
detections.threshold: 3 failures followed by success in 10 minutes for the same user and source
detections.severity_reason: A success after repeated failures is a higher-confidence sequence that should be reviewed before broad tuning.
detections.tuning: Compare with password resets, user mistakes and approved support activity.

detections.title: Account Lockout Burst
detections.threshold: 3 lockouts in 15 minutes per caller and computer
detections.severity_reason: Lockout bursts can be operational noise or abuse pressure, so ownership and affected account type drive severity.
detections.tuning: Treat as an operational anomaly; investigate stale services and machine accounts first.

detections.title: Remote Logon Followed By Special Privileges
detections.threshold: Remote or network logon followed by privileges in 2 minutes for the same logon ID
detections.severity_reason: Privileged remote sessions have higher impact and should be matched against approved administration paths.
detections.tuning: Baseline approved administration paths, jump hosts and service identities.
